stem: A food manufacturing study measures the weight (grams) for a sample of 15 cereal boxes. The sample mean is 114.1 g with a sample standard deviation of 26.4 g. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 110.8 g and standard deviation of 20.9 g. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 114.1, 95% CI = [99.5, 128.7]. Without outliers: mean = 110.8, 95% CI = [99.2, 122.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [99.2, 128.7]. Point estimate: 112.4 g.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [99.2, 128.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
